rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible or properly declared in the same annex scope.
When AGREE diagnostics report type errors, trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve or remove the clause.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be addressed by checking visibility and declaration scope.
AGREE contracts must reference only visible symbols or locally declared helpers to avoid type errors.
Minimal repair involves fixing root causes of diagnostics without altering unrelated architecture elements.
Use only features or helpers declared in the same annex when writing AGREE clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component implementation annexes are used for bindings and assignments valid in that scope.
Ensure all identifiers in a guarantee or assumption are either visible or declared locally.
anti_patterns: Adding new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type errors instead of fixing the root reference issue.
Introducing invalid or unresolved identifiers in AGREE contracts without ensuring their visibility.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees
